posts.text:
I’ve seen posts lately about the importance of critical minerals in Tomahawk missiles.

 Here is the simplified breakdown of the estimated reduction or replacement potential for each critical mineral in a tomahawk missle using Turbostratic Fractal Graphene 
 * Copper (Cu) & Silver (Ag): 95% – 100%
 * Tantalum (Ta): 95% – 100%
 * Graphite (C): 100%
 * Gallium (Ga): 80% – 100%
 * Aluminum (Al): 80% – 95%
 * Titanium (Ti): 70% – 90%
 * Vanadium (V): 70% – 90%
 * Cobalt (Co) & Molybdenum (Mo): 10% – 20%

Conclusion: $hg $hgraf is going to dramatically reduce USA reliance on critical minerals. 

@DeptofWar @ENERGY @CMCoe @RealDougCasey 

With the Middle East conflict in the news and fears about China’s domination of critical minerals supply and Iran’s success with drones.  It would seem imperative that the USA government fast track the development of a USA GEIC in Texas and partner with $hg $hgraf to innovate with Turbostratic Fractal Graphene.  It’s a matter of national security.  Further, it’s imperative for all NATO countries to upgrade military hardware with this new graphene material.
posts.author: BambroughKevin
